approach: Progressive delivery with AI features front-loaded. Each phase produces a deployable, demonstrable system. Phases are sequenced by value delivery, not waterfall stages. Timeline is relative — actual durations depend on discovery findings and team composition.

phases.name: Phase 0: Demo & Proof of Value
objectives: Build working demo that proves the AI-driven PA review concept
Validate Anthropic PA review skill capabilities with mock clinical data
Demonstrate end-to-end flow: PA submission → AI review → determination with reasoning
deliverables: CLI tool or API using Anthropic PA review skill + Claude healthcare connectors
Mock PA cases with realistic clinical data
Demo walkthrough script aligned to presentation narrative
Azure deployment (App Service or Container Apps)
success_criteria: Demo processes a PA request end-to-end with AI-generated clinical reasoning
Determination includes confidence score, evidence citations, and coverage criteria matching
Demo is accessible via URL for panel to interact with
risks: Anthropic PA review skill may not cover all demo scenarios — mitigate with mock data and focused use cases

phases.name: Phase 1: Production MVP
objectives: Deploy core PA automation for a single LOB with single integration channel
Integrate Autonomize PA Copilot with payer eligibility system
Establish confidence-based routing with human review workflow
deliverables: PA Copilot integrated with payer eligibility API
Single-channel ingestion (web portal — most structured, lowest risk)
Clinical data access via FHIR R4 endpoints (modern sources only)
Determination Router with configurable confidence threshold
Clinical Review Dashboard (Autonomize AI Studio) for human reviewers
Audit trail for all AI determinations
LLMOps dashboard with accuracy and confidence monitoring
success_criteria: PA requests processed end-to-end for single LOB
Auto-determination rate meets configurable target (starting conservative)
Human reviewers can see AI recommendations and provide feedback
Audit trail captures AI reasoning for every determination
CMS-0057-F timeline compliance demonstrated for processed requests
team_allocation: Discovery findings determine team size and composition
risks: Payer Core API latency may exceed targets
FHIR endpoint data quality may vary

phases.name: Phase 2: Scale
objectives: Add fax ingestion channel (highest volume, highest ROI)
Add legacy database connectors for clinical data
Scale to additional LOBs with per-LOB configuration
Implement advanced LLMOps: eval pipeline, drift detection, feedback loop
deliverables: Fax OCR ingestion pipeline (Azure AI Document Intelligence)
Legacy clinical database connectors
LOB configuration framework (rules engine per LOB)
Automated eval pipeline with golden test cases
Drift detection and alerting
Human feedback → eval dataset update loop
success_criteria: Fax channel processing with acceptable OCR accuracy
Multiple LOBs processing with LOB-specific routing rules
Eval pipeline running on schedule with accuracy trending
Clinical reviewer feedback systematically captured and used
team_allocation: Discovery findings determine team size and composition
dependencies: Legacy DB access credentials and schemas
risks: Fax OCR accuracy on handwritten notes
Legacy DB connector complexity varies by system
LOB rule differences may be larger than expected

phases.name: Phase 3: Enterprise
objectives: Multi-tenant vs multi-instance scaling decision (informed by Phase 2 learnings)
Advanced analytics and reporting (CMS-0057-F public metrics)
Operational maturity: runbooks, incident response, capacity planning
deliverables: EDI ingestion channel
Scaling architecture recommendation (based on actual LOB performance data)
CMS-0057-F compliance reporting dashboard
Operational runbooks and incident response procedures
Capacity planning model based on actual usage data
success_criteria: All ingestion channels operational (portal, fax, EDI)
20 LOBs supportable with documented configuration approach
CMS-0057-F compliance metrics meeting regulatory thresholds
System operational with documented support procedures
team_allocation: Discovery findings determine team size and composition
risks: Multi-tenant vs multi-instance decision may require architecture changes
EDI complexity varies by trading partner

decision_gates.name: Post-Discovery Gate
criteria: Architecture validated with actual system APIs

risk_register.risk: Autonomize platform capabilities differ from public documentation — limited API access may constrain integration options
risk_register.mitigation: Discovery phase includes platform deep-dive with Autonomize engineering. Architecture designed with abstraction layer to adapt to actual platform capabilities.

risk_register.risk: Fax OCR accuracy on handwritten clinical notes below acceptable threshold, causing high manual review rates
risk_register.mitigation: Phased fax rollout — start with printed/typed faxes, iterate on handwritten. Low-confidence OCR results routed to manual review. Azure AI Document Intelligence custom model training as backup.

risk_register.risk: LLM clinical accuracy insufficient for auto-determination at target threshold
risk_register.mitigation: Start with conservative confidence threshold (high bar for auto-approve). Continuous eval with golden test cases. Human reviewer feedback loop improves accuracy over time. Traditional ML classifier as triage tool if needed.

risk_register.risk: Legacy clinical database integration more complex than estimated — undocumented schemas, poor data quality
risk_register.mitigation: Phase 1 uses FHIR R4 endpoints only. Legacy DB integration deferred to Phase 2. Discovery phase inventories all legacy systems and data quality.

risk_register.risk: Key clinical domain expertise not available on team — incorrect coverage criteria interpretation
risk_register.mitigation: Clinical SME required for validation (not implementation). Coverage criteria maintained by clinical team, not engineering. AI eval includes clinical review sampling.

external.dependency: Autonomize AI platform access and onboarding
Payer Core System API documentation and credentials
FHIR R4 endpoint access and test data

communication_plan.stakeholder_updates: Weekly status reports to project sponsor. Sprint demos every 2 weeks. Monthly executive summary. Risk escalation as-needed.
demo_schedule: Phase 0 Demo: AI-driven PA review with mock data
Phase 1 Demo: Single-LOB end-to-end PA processing
Phase 3 Demo: Enterprise readiness + compliance reporting
